scope: diagnostic-only; targets exactly the 11 t0_parse_invalid items from the existing Gate1 new run. No bucket, strict count, or summary file was modified.

determinism_check.method: re-queried llama3.1:8b at temp=0 with identical prompt/parser/num_predict=16 (original config) for all 11 items
determinism_check.note: all 11 reproduced parse_invalid under the original config; determinism intact, no item flipped to a valid letter by chance.

step3_category.verdict: mixed — does not collapse into a single category A/B/C/D
step3_category.B_evidence: 6/11 concentrate in preference_update_reason_recall; persona-level pairing (5 of 6 affected personas have exactly 2 invalid items, traced to shared context driving near-identical model outputs across sibling items).
step3_category.A_evidence: 4/11 are genuine refusals with no letter stated even when given far more generation room (num_predict=120).
step3_category.C_evidence: Only 1/11 (bb7cf8c9) has a letter already present in the exact raw output captured under the original num_predict=16 config — the only case fixable by a parser change alone, with zero new generation.
step3_category.D_evidence: 5/11 only reveal a letter once num_predict is raised; recovering them requires new generation under a different config, not a re-parse of existing output, because the original 16-token output never contained a letter.
step3_category.blocking_issue: Step 4's required sensitivity check (apply any new parser rule to the other 578 items and confirm buckets are unchanged) cannot be performed: raw_output was never persisted for those 578 items either (same root-cause gap as the 11 invalid items). There is nothing to re-parse for them, and generating raw_output for them now would mean re-running all 589 items, which is explicitly out of scope for this diagnostic ('589件全体...には触れない').

step4_recommendation.action_taken: none — no parser change, no re-parse, no bucket/denominator change. gate1_per_item.csv, gate1_summary.json, strict_pool.csv are all unmodified.
step4_recommendation.rationale: Even the one parser-bug-possible item (bb7cf8c9) cannot be safely folded into a general rule change without the §Step4-mandated 578-item sensitivity check, which is blocked by the missing raw_output for those items. Changing the rule for 1 item only, without that check, would be exactly the 'fix the rule case-by-case to recover convenient items' pattern the user explicitly prohibited.
step4_recommendation.for_user_decision: denominator choice (valid-only 578 vs all-589 with invalid counted separately) is left open, per the constraint that this prompt does not finalize it.
step4_recommendation.process_fix_for_future_runs: raw_output must be persisted for every model call going forward (see CLAUDE.md update). A future full Gate-1-style re-run should pre-specify num_predict and a full-text (not leading-token-only) extraction rule before running, so this kind of retroactive ambiguity does not recur.
